title: astragalus crassicarpus var. paysonii
intro: Astragalus crassicarpus var. paysonii, also known as Payson's milkvetch or Payson's locoweed, is a plant species in the pea family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and its health benefits may include reducing fatigue, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Astragalus crassicarpus var. paysonii can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
